volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Woman of Andros (Section 4.5.796 - Section 5.2.855)

sections.label: Section 4.5.796
sections.original: In hac habitasse platea dictum est Chrysidem
sections.translation: It was said that Chrysis used to live in this street , who preferred to gain wealth here dishonorably to living honestly as a poor woman in her own country : by her death that property has descended to me by law . [*]

sections.label: Section 4.5.800
sections.translation: But I see some persons of whom to make inquiry . ( Accosting them .) Good-morrow to you .

sections.label: Section 4.5.800b
sections.translation: Prithee , whom do I see ? Isn ’ t this Crito , the kinsman of Chrysis ? It is he .

sections.label: Section 4.5.802b
sections.translation: O Mysis , greetings to you .

sections.label: Section 4.5.802c
sections.translation: Welcome to you , Crito .

sections.label: Section 4.5.803
sections.translation: Is Chrysis then —? [*] Alas !

sections.label: Section 4.5.803b
sections.translation: Too truly . She has indeed left us poor creatures quite heart-broken .

sections.label: Section 4.5.804
sections.original: Quid vos ? quo pacto hic ? satin recte ?
sections.translation: How fare you here , and in what fashion ? Pretty well ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.804b
sections.translation: What , we ? Just

sections.label: Section 4.5.805
sections.original: Ut quimus aiunt , quando ut volumus non licet .
sections.translation: as we can , as they say ; since we can ’ t as we would .

sections.label: Section 4.5.806
sections.original: Quid Glycerium ? iam hic suos parentes repperit ?
sections.translation: How is Glycerium ? Has she discovered her parents yet ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.807
sections.translation: I wish she had .

sections.label: Section 4.5.807b
sections.original: An nondum etiam ? Haud auspicato huc me appuli :
sections.translation: What , not yet ? With no favorable omen did I set out for this place ; for , upon my faith , if I had known that , I never would have moved a foot hither . She was always said to be , and was looked upon as her sister ;

sections.label: Section 4.5.810
sections.original: Quae illius fuerunt possidet : nunc me hospitem
sections.translation: what things were hers she is in possession of ; now for me to begin a suit at law here , the precedents of others warn me , a stranger , [*] how easy and profitable a task it would be for me . At the same time , I suppose that by this she has got some friend and protector ; for she was pretty nearly a grown-up girl when she left there . They would cry out

sections.label: Section 4.5.815
sections.translation: that I am a sharper ; that , a pauper , I ’ m hunting after an inheritance ; besides , I shouldn ’ t like to strip the girl herself .

sections.label: Section 4.5.817
sections.original: O optime hospes pol Crito antiquum obtines .
sections.translation: O most worthy stranger ! I ’ faith , Crito , you still adhere to your good old-fashioned ways .

sections.label: Section 4.5.818
sections.original: Duc me ad eam , quando huc veni , ut videam .
sections.translation: Lead me to her , since I have come hither , that I may see her .

sections.label: Section 4.5.818b
sections.translation: By all means .

sections.label: Section 4.5.819
sections.original: Sequar hos : nolo me in tempore hoc videat senex .
sections.translation: I ’ ll follow them ; I don ’ t wish the old man to see me at this moment .

sections.label: Section 5.1.820
sections.original: Satis iam satis Simo spectata erga te amicitia est mea :
sections.translation: Enough already , enough , Simo , has my friendship toward you been proved . Sufficient hazard have I begun to encounter ; make an end of your entreaties , then . While I ’ ve been endeavoring to oblige you , I ’ ve almost fooled away my daughter ’ s prospects in life .

sections.label: Section 5.1.823
sections.original: Immo enim nunc quum maxime abs te postulo atque oro Chreme
sections.translation: Nay but , now in especial , Chremes , I do beg and entreat of you , that the favor , commenced a short time since in words , you ’ ll now complete by deeds .

sections.label: Section 5.1.825
sections.original: Vide quam iniquus sis prae studio . Dum id efficias quod cupis ,
sections.translation: See how unreasonable you are from your very earnestness ; so long as you effect what you desire , you neither think of limits to compliance , nor what it is you request of me ; for if you did think , you would now forbear to trouble me with unreasonable requests .

sections.label: Section 5.1.828
sections.translation: What unreasonable requests ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.828b
sections.original: Ah ! rogitas ? perpulisti me ut homini adolescentulo
sections.translation: Do you ask ? You importuned me to promise my daughter to a young man engaged in another attachment , averse to the marriage state ,

sections.label: Section 5.1.830
sections.original: Filiam darem in seditionem atque in incertas nuptias ,
sections.translation: to plunge her into discord and a marriage of uncertain duration ; that through her sorrow and her anguish I might reclaim your son . You prevailed ; while the case admitted of it I made preparations . Now it does not admit of it ; you must put up with it ; they say that she is a citizen of this place ; a child has been born ; do cease to trouble us .

sections.label: Section 5.1.834
sections.original: Per ego te deos oro ut ne illis animum inducas credere
sections.translation: By the Gods , I do conjure you not to bring your mind to believe

sections.label: Section 5.1.835
sections.original: Quibus id maxime utile est illum esse quam deterrimum .
sections.translation: those whose especial interest it is that he should be as degraded as possible . On account of the marriage , have all these things been feigned and contrived . When the reason for which they do these things is removed from them , they will desist .

sections.label: Section 5.1.838
sections.original: Erras : cum Davo egomet vidi iurgantem ancillam .
sections.translation: You are mistaken ; I myself saw the servant-maid wrangling with Davus .

sections.label: Section 5.1.838b
sections.translation: I am aware .

sections.label: Section 5.1.838c
sections.translation: With an appearance of earnestness , when neither at the moment perceived that I was present there .

sections.label: Section 5.1.840
sections.original: Credo ; et id facturas Davus dudum praedixit mihi :
sections.translation: I believe it ; and Davus a short time since forewarned me that this would be the case ; and I don ’ t know how I forgot to tell it you to-day , as I had intended .

sections.label: Section 5.2.842
sections.original: Animo nunc iam otioso esse impero —
sections.translation: Now then , I bid you set your minds at ease .

sections.label: Section 5.2.842b
sections.translation: See you , there ’ s Davus .

sections.label: Section 5.2.843
sections.translation: From what house is he coming out ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.843b
sections.translation: Through my means , and that of the stranger —

sections.label: Section 5.2.843c
sections.translation: What mischief is this ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.844
sections.original: Ego commodiorem hominem , adventum , tempus non vidi .
sections.translation: I never did see a more opportune person , encounter , or occasion .

sections.label: Section 5.2.844b
sections.translation: The rascal !

sections.label: Section 5.2.845
sections.translation: I wonder who it is he ’ s praising ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.845b
sections.original: Omnis res est iam in vado .
sections.translation: All the affair is now in a safe position .

sections.label: Section 5.2.845c
sections.translation: Why do I delay to accost him ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.846
sections.translation: It ’ s my master ; what am I to do ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.846b
sections.translation: O , save you , good sir !

sections.label: Section 5.2.846c
sections.translation: Hah ! Simo ! O , Chremes , my dear sir , all things are now quite ready in-doors .

sections.label: Section 5.2.847b
sections.translation: You have taken such very good care .

sections.label: Section 5.2.848
sections.translation: Send for the bride when you like .

sections.label: Section 5.2.848b
sections.original: Bene sane ; id enimvero hinc nunc abest .
sections.translation: Very good : ( ironically .) of course , that ’ s the only thing that ’ s now wanting here . But do you answer me this , what business had you there ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.849b
sections.translation: What , I ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.850
sections.translation: Just so .

sections.label: Section 5.2.850b
sections.translation: I ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.850c
sections.translation: Yes , you .

sections.label: Section 5.2.850d
sections.translation: I went in just now .

sections.label: Section 5.2.850e
sections.translation: As if I asked how long ago !

sections.label: Section 5.2.851
sections.translation: Together with your son .

sections.label: Section 5.2.851b
sections.original: Anne est intus Pamphilus ? crucior miser .
sections.translation: What , is Phamphilus in there ? ( Aside .) To my confusion , I ’ m on the rack ! ( To DAVUS .) How now ? Didn ’ t you say that there was enmity between them , you scoundrel ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.853
sections.translation: There is .

sections.label: Section 5.2.853b
sections.translation: Why is he there , then ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.853c
sections.original: Quid illum census ? cum illa litigat .
sections.translation: Why do you . suppose he is ? ( Ironically .) Quarreling with her , of course .

sections.label: Section 5.2.854
sections.original: Immo vero indignum Chreme iam facinus faxo ex me audias .
sections.translation: Nay but , Chremes , I ’ ll let you now hear from me a disgraceful piece of business .

sections.label: Section 5.2.855
sections.original: Nescio quis senex modo venit : ellum confidens , catus .
sections.translation: An old man , I don ’ t know who he is , has just now come here ; look you , he is a confident and shrewd person ; when you look at his appearance , he seems to be a person of some consequence . There is a grave sternness in his features , and something commanding in his words .
